requirementCueLines: [Page 1] The Bachelor of Arts in Architectural Studies is a four-year, GENERAL EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS
[Page 1] undergraduate degree program in which students explore the 5cr English Composition (ENGL 131 Preferred)
[Page 1] many factors that shape our built environment. The program 66cr Total in Areas of Inquiry
[Page 1] provides a liberal studies approach to the study of architecture 20cr Arts & Humanities (A&H)
[Page 1] that covers spatial reasoning, aesthetics, political and economic 20cr Social Sciences (SSc)
[Page 1] structures with attention to social justice and equity, socio- 20cr Natural Sciences (NSc)
[Page 1] through a sequence of courses that allows concentration in one YEAR TWO REQUIRED ARCH COURSES
[Page 1] 3cr ARCH 350: Architecture of the Ancient World (au)
[Page 1] 3cr ARCH 351: Arch of Medieval & Early Mod World (wi)
[Page 1] Before graduation, students must also complete two Writing
[Page 1] (W) courses for a minimum total of 10 credits, and 5 credits of
[Page 1] Diversity (DIV) course(s). These courses may also count toward
[Page 1] any other requirement, except the 5-credit English composition
[Page 1] requirement. Students should complete some or all of these
[Page 1] Architecture’s BA program. Students must complete prerequisite
[Page 1] requirements prior to admission to the Architecture program.
[Page 1] The complete Departmental application for sophomores, juniors,
[Page 1] have completed at least 45 credits by the time of admission recommended.
[Page 1] into the program, including Year Two required courses. The
[Page 1] application deadline for sophomore admission is the first day of YEAR 3 + 4 REQUIREMENTS
[Page 1] required courses and a minimum of 90 credits. The application
[Page 1] Admission decisions are based on an applicant’s academic 6cr ARCH 469: Architecture Capstone*
[Page 1] performance and potential, extent and quality of relevant 3 Area of Concentration selective courses from
[Page 1] one from each area of concentration (minumum 9cr)
[Page 1] 2 College of Built Environment elective courses
